title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
problem_statement: Remove duplicates in-place from a sorted array and return the length of the unique portion.

examples.explanation: The first five elements become [0,1,2,3,4] after removal.

constraints: nums is sorted in non-decreasing order.
intuition: Use a slow pointer for placement and a fast pointer to scan duplicates.

approaches.name: Two-pointer overwrite
approaches.description: Keep one pointer for the next unique insert position and one pointer to scan through the array.
approaches.why_not: Using a set preserves order poorly and uses extra space.

interview_qa.q: What does the slow pointer represent?
interview_qa.a: The position of the last unique element kept so far.
Because the array is sorted, duplicate values are contiguous.
